msgs.text:
According to the current attached history document from 2026-06-28, Turn 121 is another attachment-only continuation: the current inputs are still mega_prompt.md, problem_statement.md, and turns_history__q2_candidate_iso.md, with no new object-level nonlinear Certificate D artifacts attached.

Turn 121 — attachment-only idempotence remains closed

The state remains unchanged from Turn 120:(THEOREM_STATE,MEASURED_CERTIFICATE_D_STATE)=(ΓS≅GΓH, NO_MEASURED_INPUT).

The problem asks whether there is a continuous isomorphismΦ:ΓS∼ΓHwithπH∘Φ=πS,i.e. an isomorphism over the fixed quotient G.

The problem statement defines ΓS,ΓH, their canonical quotient maps to G, and exactly this over-G condition.The theorem coordinate remains closed inside the attached sweep: the history records the stronger statement that there is a continuous over-G automorphism Φ:F=P⋊G⟶F=P⋊G such thatΦ(rS−1)=rH.

Since rS and rS−1 generate the same closed normal subgroup of F, this descends to

F/⟨⟨rS⟩⟩F≅G

F/⟨⟨rH⟩⟩F,henceΓS≅GΓH.The attached history records this as the final theorem-level state and explains the quotient descent.No structural computation should be reopened under the current inputs. The frozen proof chain remainsH1 -Nielsen equivalence+ residual restricted-Lie induction+ normalized Koszul–Witt coefficient induction+ Certificates D.1–D.4+ finite filtered Nielsen compactness⟹ΓS≅GΓH.The compactness step remains the corrected filtered-Nielsen one: for coefficient-and-degree truncations PN,J, the finite sets SN,J consist of filtered Nielsen/cocycle data, not arbitrary quotient automorphisms; their compatible inverse limit gives an over-G automorphism Φ:F→F, and intersecting all PN,J gives Φ(rS−1)=rH.The measured-certificate coordinate also remains unchanged. The current attachments do not include genuine object-level nonlinear Certificate D artifacts: no D.1–D.4 scripts, matrices, pivot tables, or measured-output logs independent of the history ledger. Therefore the strict Certificate D gate remains

NO_MEASURED_INPUT,not PASS, not FAIL, and not REJECTED_AS_LEDGER_DERIVED. The history’s recorded verifier claims support the theorem coordinate, but they are not newly attached measured artifacts for the strict gate.

So the attachment-only transition rule applies again:{mega_prompt.md,problem_statement.md,turns_history__q2_candidate_iso.md}⟹(ΓS≅GΓH, NO_MEASURED_INPUT).

ESTABLISHED: Turn 121 is idempotent under the same attachment-only conditions as Turn 120. The theorem coordinate remains ΓS≅GΓH, via an over-G automorphism Φ:F→F satisfying Φ(rS−1)=rH. The strict measured Certificate D coordinate remains NO_MEASURED_INPUT.

GAP: No structural mathematical gap is reopened. The only remaining gap is external reproducibility: no genuine object-level measured nonlinear D.1–D.4 artifacts are attached in this turn.

NEXT: Keep the obstruction sweep closed. The only non-idempotent next move is to attach genuine measured D.1–D.4 scripts, matrices, pivots, or measured-output logs not derived from the history, then run the strict gate as PASS or FAIL.[TURN121-ATTACHMENT-ONLY-IDEMPOTENT-GATE]
